# TopGun

TopGun is a hybrid offline-first in-memory data grid — zero-latency reads/writes via local CRDTs, real-time sync via WebSockets, durable storage on PostgreSQL or redb.

## Docs

- [Introduction](https://topgun.build/intro/): What TopGun is, how it bridges in-memory data grids and offline-first client databases, and when to use it.
- [Quick Start](https://topgun.build/quick-start/): Build your first local-first application with TopGun in under 5 minutes — scaffold, connect, and sync.
- [Core Concepts](https://topgun.build/concepts/): Understand the distributed systems theory behind TopGun: local-first architecture, CRDTs, Hybrid Logical Clocks, and the sync protocol.
- [Schema & Type Safety](https://topgun.build/guides/schema/): Add TypeScript type safety to TopGun maps using the TopGun<T> generic class.
- [MCP Server](https://topgun.build/guides/mcp-server/): Connect AI assistants like Claude Desktop and Cursor to your TopGun database using the Model Context Protocol.
- [AI Builder Guide](https://topgun.build/guides/building-with-ai/): Build a TopGun app using Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex — prompt templates, common pitfalls, and live database access via MCP.
- [API Reference](https://topgun.build/reference/): Full reference for TopGunClient, React hooks, data structures, server API, and the wire protocol.
- [Comparison](https://topgun.build/comparison/): How TopGun compares to Firebase, Supabase Realtime, Replicache, and Yjs — trade-offs, migration paths, and positioning.

## Optional

- [FAQ](https://topgun.build/faq/): Frequently asked questions about TopGun's architecture, hosting, and use cases.
- [Benchmarks](https://topgun.build/benchmarks/): Measured throughput and latency numbers — 560 K ops/sec fire-and-forget, 37 K ops/sec fire-and-wait at 1.5 ms p50 on M1 Max.
- [Roadmap](https://topgun.build/roadmap/): Upcoming features and the post-launch engineering plan.

For full docs concatenated into one file, see [/llms-full.txt](https://topgun.build/llms-full.txt).
